title: Root Cause Analysis
description: Systematically find the true root cause of a bug, not just a symptom.
content:
I have this bug: {{symptom}}

Help me find the root cause, not just a patch. Work through it:
1. Restate the expected vs. actual behavior precisely.
2. List the most likely hypotheses, ordered by probability.
3. For each, name the single cheapest observation that would confirm or rule it out.
4. Tell me exactly what to inspect, log, or run next.

Do not propose a fix until we've isolated the cause. Ask for any log, stack trace, or code you need.

title: Explain This Error
description: Decode a stack trace or error message and pinpoint the fix.
content:
Explain this error and how to fix it:

```
{{error}}
```

Tell me:
1. In plain language, what the error actually means.
2. The most likely cause given the trace.
3. The specific line/condition to look at first.
4. The minimal fix, and how to verify it's gone.

If the trace is ambiguous, list the top 2-3 causes and how to tell them apart.

variables.name: error
variables.description: The error message or stack trace

title: Add Strategic Logging
description: Instrument code to capture exactly the evidence a bug requires.
content:
I'm chasing this intermittent bug: {{bug}}

Show me where to add logging to capture the evidence needed to diagnose it. For each log point:
- What to log (state, inputs, timing) and at what level.
- Why that data discriminates between hypotheses.
- How to keep it cheap enough to leave in production if useful.

Prefer a few high-signal logs over blanket tracing.

title: Minimal Reproduction
description: Reduce a bug to the smallest reliable reproduction.
content:
Help me build a minimal reproduction of this bug: {{bug}}

Guide me to strip it down:
1. What to remove first to test whether it still reproduces.
2. How to replace external dependencies with fakes/stubs.
3. How to make it deterministic (seed randomness, pin time, fix ordering).

The goal is the smallest self-contained snippet that fails every time. Tell me what to try next based on what I report.

title: Why Is This Slow?
description: Diagnose a performance problem before optimizing.
content:
This is slow: {{what}}

Before changing anything, help me locate the bottleneck:
1. What to measure and how (profiler, timing, counters).
2. The usual suspects for this kind of workload, ranked.
3. How to confirm the bottleneck is what we think before optimizing.

Once we've measured, propose the highest-leverage fix and predict its impact. Don't guess-optimize.

title: Debug Failing Test
description: Figure out why a specific test fails and whether the test or the code is wrong.
content:
This test is failing:

```
{{test_and_output}}
```

Determine:
1. Is the test asserting the right thing, or is the test itself wrong?
2. What is the actual vs. expected value, and where they diverge.
3. The smallest change that makes it pass for the *right* reason.
4. Whether it might be flaky (timing, ordering, shared state) rather than a real failure.

variables.name: test_and_output
variables.description: The test code and its failure output

title: Bisect a Regression
description: Track down which change introduced a regression.
content:
Something that used to work now breaks: {{regression}}

Help me bisect it:
1. Define a crisp pass/fail test I can run at any commit.
2. Walk me through using git bisect (or manual halving) to find the culprit commit.
3. Once found, explain how to read that diff to understand WHY it broke things.
4. Recommend a fix that doesn't reintroduce whatever the bad commit was trying to do.

title: Heisenbug Hunt
description: Approach a bug that disappears under observation or happens only sometimes.
content:
I have an intermittent bug that's hard to pin down: {{bug}}

Treat it as a non-determinism problem. Help me:
1. Enumerate sources of non-determinism (threads, I/O ordering, time, randomness, uninitialized memory, network).
2. Pick the cheapest way to make each source observable or controllable.
3. Design a stress harness that makes it reproduce more often.

Give me a concrete next experiment, not general advice.

title: Diagnose a Memory Leak
description: Find what is retaining memory that should be freed.
content:
Memory keeps growing in: {{context}}

Help me find the leak. Tell me what to capture (heap snapshots, retained-size comparison over time) and how to read it. The usual suspects: unbounded caches or maps, event listeners never removed, closures capturing large scopes, growing arrays. For each, how to confirm and fix. Give me the next concrete measurement to take.

title: Diagnose a Deadlock
content:
My program hangs here: {{symptom}}

Walk me through diagnosing a deadlock: how to capture the state of all threads or tasks and see who is waiting on whom. Identify the lock-ordering or wait-cycle causing it. Propose a fix that breaks the cycle (consistent lock order, timeouts, lock-free, or removing the shared lock). Explain why your fix cannot reintroduce the deadlock.

title: Compare Expected vs Actual
description: Systematically find where two outputs diverge.

title: Validate an Assumption
description: Test a belief you are relying on while debugging.
content:
While debugging, I am assuming: {{assumption}}

This might be wrong. Tell me the cheapest way to actually verify it (a log, an assert, a one-line check, a debugger watch). Do not let me keep building on an unverified assumption. If it is false, what does that immediately imply about where the bug really is?

variables.name: assumption
variables.description: The belief you are relying on
